questions.q: What is the basic difference between Monera and Protista?
questions.answer: Monera are prokaryotic organisms, whereas Protista are eukaryotic organisms. They are unicellular and lack a true nucleus. Monera are autotrophic and obtain their energy from sunlight, whereas Protista are heterotrophic and may be autotrophic or heterotrophic.

questions.q: What is the process of obtaining food by engulfing and digesting particles in the environment?
questions.answer: The process is called phagotrophy. This mode of nutrition is characteristic of Protista and some other eukaryotic organisms.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of reproduction that involves the production of spores?
questions.answer: The process is called asexual reproduction, specifically spore formation. It is a mode of reproduction in which the organisms produce spores to reproduce.

questions.q: What are the two main types of symbiotic relationships?
questions.answer: The two main types of symbiotic relationships are mutualism and parasitism. Mutualism is a relationship in which both organisms benefit, whereas parasitism is a relationship in which one organism benefits at the expense of the other.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of obtaining food by capturing and eating other organisms?
questions.answer: The process is called predation. This mode of nutrition is characteristic of carnivorous organisms.

questions.q: What is the term for the study of the diversity of life on Earth?
questions.answer: The study is called taxonomy. It involves the classification, identification, and naming of organisms.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of reproduction in which the parent organism produces offspring that are genetically identical?
questions.answer: The process is called asexual reproduction. It is a mode of reproduction in which the organisms produce offspring through mitosis or binary fission.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of obtaining food by using sunlight to synthesize organic compounds?
questions.answer: The process is called autotrophy. It is a mode of nutrition in which the organisms produce their own food using sunlight.
key_points: Using sunlight to synthesize organic compounds

questions.q: What is the term for the study of the structure, function, and development of organisms?
questions.answer: The study is called morphology. It involves the examination of the physical characteristics of organisms.

questions.q: What is the term for the process of reproduction in which the parent organism produces offspring that are genetically diverse?
questions.answer: The process is called sexual reproduction. It is a mode of reproduction in which the organisms produce offspring through the fusion of gametes.
